data.message: Completed sc-business-logic + sc-jwt security scan of UWAS codebase. Key findings: 3 Critical, 6 High, 6 Medium, 3 Low. Primary issue is missing authorization checks on ~80+ API endpoints where any authenticated user (including RoleUser) can perform administrative actions. No JWT implementation found (session-based auth). Report written to security-report/sc-business-logic-results.md.

data.message: Security scan completed for UWAS v0.0.55. Key findings: SQL injection mitigated via escapeSQL/BacktickID (not parameterized queries), timing-safe API key comparison via subtle.ConstantTimeCompare, XFF spoofing protection via rightmost-untrusted IP extraction, path traversal prevented via IsWithinBaseResolved symlink resolver, command injection mitigated via validateShellCommand and shellQuote. Admin-only cron execute endpoint confirmed. Dependencies are minimal (5 direct deps) with no known vulnerabilities.

git commit -m "fix(dashboard): guard null and paginated API responses to prevent UI crashes" -m "Several backend endpoints moved to the paginated envelope but the frontend kept fetching them as plain T[]; calling .map/.filter on the envelope object crashed the page on mount." -m "api.ts: extract .items (with fallback) for fetchAuditLog, fetchServices, fetchWebhooks, fetchPackages, fetchTasks, fetchApps, fetchDeploys, fetchFiles. Null guards in DNS.tsx (server_ips), Doctor.tsx (checks), DBExplorer.tsx (databases/tables/columns), Cache.tsx (domains). Verified with tsc -b." 2>&1

git commit -m "fix(dashboard): default array endpoints to [] when backend returns null" -m "Go nil slices marshal to JSON null. Any api<T[]>() call where the backend returns nil would crash the consumer page on .map/.filter. Adds .then(r => r ?? []) to all 16 array endpoints (logs, certs, analytics, unknown-domains, ssh-keys, db-users, admin-users, bandwidth, cron monitor, security blocked, wp users, cloudflare tunnels/zones, db tables/columns, php domains)." 2>&1

data.code: git commit -m "fix(dashboard): rebuild embedded bundle with all api.ts safety fixes" -m "The embedded internal/admin/dashboard/dist was last rebuilt at commit 03f2541, before the paginated-items extract and ?? [] guards landed. Production binaries (including v0.1.1) were therefore still shipping the broken bundle that crashed with H.filter is not a function on Dashboard mount when /api/v1/certs etc. returned the new envelope shape." -m "Rebuilds dist (vite v8.0.3, 2452 modules) so Dashboard-*.js, api-*.js and index-*.js carry the fixes." -m "Also makes release.yml release job build the dashboard before go build so embed always matches source. Previously only the validate job built the dashboard, and its output was not reused by release." 2>&1

git log -1 --oneline && git tag -a v0.1.2 -m "v0.1.2 - rebuild embedded dashboard with crash fixes" -m "v0.1.1 shipped a stale embedded bundle (Dashboard-C2ZgB-KS.js) that pre-dated the api.ts paginated/null fixes, so production crashed with 'H.filter is not a function' on Dashboard mount." -m "This release rebuilds dist (Dashboard-C07YkSKF.js, api-zXeCxGew.js) and updates release.yml so the release job builds the dashboard before go build, preventing this drift in the future." && git tag -n2 v0.1.2

data.code: git add internal/wordpress/installer.go internal/wordpress/installer_test.go && git diff --cached --stat

git commit -m "fix(wordpress): use SHA1 checksum endpoint, not nonexistent SHA256" -m "wordpress.org publishes latest.tar.gz.sha1 and .md5 but never .sha256. The installer was hitting /latest.tar.gz.sha256 which 404s into an HTML page; the parser then took 'expected = first whitespace-token of body' which evaluated to the literal string '<!DOCTYPE', causing every install to fail with 'WordPress checksum mismatch: expected <!DOCTYPE, got <real-sha>'." -m "Switches to .sha1, hashes the tarball with crypto/sha1, and adds defensive guards (HTTP 200 only, body must be a 40-char lowercase hex string) so a future endpoint change can't trip the parser the same way again. Behavior on missing/broken checksum file remains best-effort: skip verification, continue install." 2>&1

git tag -a v0.1.3 -m "v0.1.3 - WordPress installer SHA1 checksum fix" -m "wordpress.org never published .sha256 -- installer was 404'ing and parsing HTML as the expected hash, breaking every install. Switches to .sha1 with defensive validation." && git push origin main v0.1.3 2>&1

data.code: git add internal/admin/handlers_hosting.go && git commit -m "fix(selfupdate): log auto-restart failure instead of swallowing it" -m "handleUpdate fired RestartSelf in a goroutine and ignored its error. When systemctl restart and syscall.Exec both fail (e.g. UWAS not running under systemd as 'uwas', or exec denied) the API still answered 'updated' but the running process kept executing the old binary. Operators saw version unchanged and assumed the update silently broke." -m "Now logs an error with a concrete remediation hint ('sudo systemctl restart uwas' / 'sudo uwas restart') so the situation surfaces in the journal/log." 2>&1

git tag -a v0.1.4 -m "v0.1.4 - surface self-update auto-restart failures" -m "When the post-update RestartSelf step silently fails (no systemd unit named 'uwas', or syscall.Exec denied), the API used to respond 'updated' but the process kept running the old binary. v0.1.4 logs the failure with a remediation hint so operators see why they need a manual restart." && git push origin main v0.1.4 2>&1

tags: server version, v0.1.4 workflow, latest release

data.code: git add internal/terminal/handler.go internal/terminal/handler_test.go && git commit -m "fix(terminal): allow http same-origin WebSocket (was https-only, broke http deployments)" -m "CheckOrigin's same-origin fallback rejected any Origin whose scheme was not https. UWAS frequently runs on plain HTTP (internal panels, IP-only access, port 9443), so a browser loading the dashboard over http://host:9443 sent Origin: http://... and the WebSocket upgrade was refused with 'NS_ERROR_WEBSOCKET_CONNECTION_REFUSED' -- the web Terminal page never connected." -m "Drops the scheme check from the same-origin fallback. CSWSH protection still holds via host equality (Origin.Host == request.Host). For deployments that want to enforce https, set AllowedOrigin explicitly (the AllowedOrigin path still does strict scheme + host match)." 2>&1

git tag -a v0.1.5 -m "v0.1.5 - terminal WebSocket allows http same-origin" -m "Browser-based Terminal page failed to connect on http deployments (NS_ERROR_WEBSOCKET_CONNECTION_REFUSED) because CheckOrigin enforced https-only. Now allows http same-origin while keeping CSWSH host-equality protection." && git push origin main v0.1.5 2>&1
